results.response_text:
There are several individuals named Evan Huang who work as software engineers or in related technical fields.

One Evan Huang is a software engineer and solo developer who focuses on building "living systems" and real-time tooling. This individual is currently pursuing an MS in Computer Science at New York University (2023-2025) and previously earned a BA in Computer Science & Economics from the same university (2019-2023). They are known for projects such as "World Engine," an LLM-driven world for autonomous agents.

Another Evan Huang is a former Frontend Developer with expertise in React, Next.js, and TypeScript. This Evan Huang is currently dedicating time to studies but remains passionate about coding and problem-solving. They are the founder of Tech-Art Studio, a community-driven web development studio, and have developed projects like "One Calendar," a privacy-first calendar web app, and "Mail," an email sending application. This developer uses "She/Her" pronouns.

A third individual, Evan Huang, works as a Full Stack Data Scientist at Quantifind, a role they have held since October 2022, after previously serving as a Data Scientist at the same company from April 2019 to February 2022. This Evan Huang has a strong academic background, including a Bachelor of Science and Engineering and a Master's Degree in Systems Sciences and Engineering from the University of Pennsylvania (2013-2017), and a Master's Degree in Electrical Engineering from Stanford University (2017-2019), where they focused on AI, Stochastic Systems, and Deep Learning. Their experience also includes research and teaching roles at the University of Pennsylvania and an internship at AQR Capital Management.

results.response_text:
Depending on the specific company or industry context, "Evan Huang" most commonly refers to one of several prominent technology and software leaders:
Evan Huang (Director of Engineering at HubSpot): Based in the Boston area, he is a prominent engineering leader who previously spent five years at Airbnb driving core search and personalized recommendations. He focuses heavily on integrating AI/ML into products, CRM automation, and managing distributed engineering teams.
Evan Huang (Director of Software Engineering at Google): Based in the San Jose/Silicon Valley area, he has been with Google for over 15 years, moving from Senior Staff Software Engineer to Director. He specializes in distributed systems, big data, and machine learning, with prior experience at PayPal.
Evan Huang (Software Engineer at Google): A UC Berkeley computer science graduate who joined Google as a Software Engineer after interning at Eikon Therapeutics and Securitizer.io.
If you can share which company, location, or project you are most interested in, I can give you a more targeted profile for that specific Evan Huang.

results.response_text:
It appears there are several individuals named Evan Huang, each with their own distinct projects. Here's a breakdown of what different Evan Huangs have built:

**Evan Huang (Developer - evanqhuang.com & GitHub evanqhuang)**
This Evan Huang focuses on distributed systems, developer tooling, and underwater hardware. His projects include:
*   **HostelHawk** — A price-history tracker for Hostelworld inventory, built with SvelteKit, Rust, and TimescaleDB.
*   **Camazotz** — Involves designing and selling cave diving equipment, including a handheld underwater cave-mapping device with real-time navigation, using Fusion and RP2350 firmware in C.
*   **FinFeed** — A live fish-tank camera streamed to any browser over HLS without a cloud service, utilizing Raspberry Pi, FFmpeg, and hls.js.
*   **geo-daemon** — A weekly tracker for brand visibility across AI search engines, developed with TypeScript.
*   **maxwells-razor** — A modular, 90% 3D printed, 3D printer toolchanger.
*   **camazotz-ixchel** — RP2350 firmware for a handheld underwater cave mapping device.
*   **dbm3** — A slab diffusion decompression model backtested against Buhlmann ZH-L16C.
*   **T480s-hackintosh** — A Clover EFI folder for a T480s running Catalina 10.15.7.

**Evan Huang (Developer - EvanTechDev & Tech-Art Studio)**
This Evan Huang is a developer who has built:
*   **One Calendar** — A privacy-first calendar web app built with Next.js, featuring modern security like end-to-end encryption, password-protected sharing, and self-destructing share links.
*   **Mail** — A resend email sending web application built with NextJS and password protection.
*   **Portfolio** — His personal portfolio website, built with Next.js.
*   **Tech-Art Docs** — Help documentation for Tech-Art Studio.

**Evan Huang (Student Inventor)**
This Evan Huang, a Year 12 student, is known for:
*   **DrinkBot** — A robot designed to grab and deliver drinks and old cups for the elderly and disabled. He created a second prototype with obstacle-detecting sensors.
*   **VEX Robotics Challenge** — He is a VEX Robotics Challenge World Winner and represented New Zealand at the World Championship.
*   **Rise NZ club** — Founded in 2022 to support young people in New Zealand in creating their own "Rise projects."

**Evan Huang (Product Designer & AI Prototyping)**
As a product designer, this Evan Huang has a portfolio including:
*   **Unmanned Golf Booking Platform**.
*   **Funeral Flower Ordering Automation Platform**.
*   **Connected** — A social media side project.
*   **One Gobo**.
*   **One Gobo CRM**.

**Evan Huang (Real Estate and Investment)**
This Evan Huang is involved in the real estate and investment sectors:
*   **Velocity Capital Group** — Co-founder and managing director of a private-equity investment firm focused on multi-family investments.
*   **Batu Capital** — Managing Partner leading investments in real estate, consumer goods, health, and fintech.
*   **Baohaus** — Co-founded this critically acclaimed restaurant.

**Evan Huang (TECC Academy)**
This Evan Huang is currently building on the legacy of **TECC Academy**, an afterschool and summer program for K-8 students in the San Gabriel Valley, which his parents purchased in 2015 and he began taking over in 2023.

**Evan Huang (from "Fresh Off the Boat" Wiki)**
In the context of the TV show, Evan Huang built a contraption that breaks down Dr. Pepper into its 23 individual flavors for a school science fair.

results.response_text:
Because there are several notable individuals named Evan Huang, here are the specific projects built by the most prominent people with this name:
Evan Huang (Director & Real Estate Developer in NYC)
Based in New York, this Evan Huang leads business development and sales for Charney Companies
and heads "The Huang Team". He has built a portfolio of public-facing real estate and neighborhood projects in the city, including:
Union Channel: A major mixed-use real estate development in the Gowanus neighborhood of Brooklyn.
The Copper:
A luxury residential development on the East River in Manhattan featuring a skyline sky-bridge, amenity decks, and connected towers.
Madison Square Park Tower: A prominent luxury residential building located in the Flatiron district of Manhattan.
Evan Huang (Software, Tech & Health AI Entrepreneur)
This Evan Huang has built several major enterprise systems and health-tech platforms:
CareSignal: A virtual care management platform acquired by Lightbeam Health Solutions that serves over 1% of the U.S. adult population.
Poplar Homes:
A tech-enabled property management and leasing platform he co-founded before it was acquired by Evernest.
Robot Health & Lightbeam Health Solutions: Custom clinical intervention AI architectures and scalable physical AI tools.
Evan Huang (Deaf Graphic Designer & Technologist in NZ)
An award-winning designer and advocate for the Deaf community in New Zealand, his passion projects include:
Sign It Out: An interactive game utilizing illustrated characters to teach New Zealand Sign Language (NZSL) to children.
DEAF:
A publication that explores and builds awareness around Deaf culture and NZSL.
Reo Ringa: An accessible booking platform concept specifically designed to help the Deaf community connect with sign language interpreters.
Evan Huang (Student & Robotics Advocate in NZ/US)
An acclaimed engineering and STEM student, his personal hardware and tech projects include:
DrinkBot: A robotic prototype equipped with obstacle-detection sensors designed to assist elderly and disabled individuals.
MoonBots VEX 862:
Founding and coaching a robotics club in Auckland, NZ, to make STEM education accessible for young learners.
Rise NZ Club: Building a local club in New Zealand to support and fund student-driven community projects.
Could you tell me which Evan Huang you are most interested in (e.g., the real estate developer, the tech entrepreneur, the student, or the designer) so I can provide more details?

queries.prompt: What is Evan Huang's underwater cave mapping project?

results.response_text:
Evan Huang is involved in an underwater cave mapping project called **Camazotz**, where he is designing and selling a handheld underwater cave-mapping device with real-time navigation capabilities.

The Camazotz project focuses on developing specialized cave diving equipment. The device utilizes Fusion and RP2350 firmware written in C, which enables live "stick map" rendering from integrated encoder, IMU (Inertial Measurement Unit), and depth sensor fusion. This technology aims to provide divers with immediate navigational feedback while exploring underwater cave systems.

Beyond his direct work on Camazotz, Evan Huang's broader interests include distributed systems, developer tooling, and occasional underwater hardware development. While other projects like "CavePI," an Autonomous Underwater Vehicle (AUV) for cave exploration, are mentioned in related research, the Camazotz project is directly attributed to Evan Huang's personal endeavors in creating a handheld mapping solution for underwater cave environments.

results.response_text:
Evanqhuang.com is the personal website of Evan Huang, a developer specializing in distributed systems, developer tooling, and underwater hardware.

His website likely serves as a showcase for his various projects and expertise. Some of the projects he is actively building include:
*   **HostelHawk**: A price-history tracker designed for Hostelworld inventory, utilizing SvelteKit, Rust, and TimescaleDB.
*   **Camazotz**: A project focused on designing and selling cave diving equipment, including a handheld underwater cave-mapping device with real-time navigation, with firmware developed in C.
*   **FinFeed**: A system for live fish-tank camera streaming to any browser over HLS, without relying on cloud services, built with Raspberry Pi, FFmpeg, and hls.js.
*   **geo-daemon**: A weekly tracker for brand visibility across AI search engines, implemented using TypeScript.

Evan Huang's technical stack includes Go, TypeScript, Python, Rust, React, Svelte, Kafka, and AWS. His GitHub profile, also under the username "evanqhuang," further details some of his pinned public repositories, such as "maxwells-razor" (a 90% 3D printed toolchanger), "camazotz-ixchel" (RP2350 firmware for an underwater cave mapping device), "dbm3" (a slab diffusion decompression model), and "T480s-hackintosh" (Clover EFI for a T480s laptop).

results.response_text:
Several individuals and communities are actively building DIY (Do-It-Yourself) underwater cave mapping hardware.

One notable individual, known as "9nl," has developed a DIY sensor system capable of mapping caves in 3D. This system integrates a Raspberry Pi 5 running ROS2, a Velodyne VLP 16 lidar sensor, and an Inertial Measurement Unit (IMU) to create detailed three-dimensional maps.

Another individual, Phil, has created an open-source tool for cave surveying, as showcased on element14 Community. His device incorporates an nrf52840 processor, an accelerometer, and an RM3100 magnetometer for accurate measurements.

Addison Snyder is also engaged in building a "DIY LiDAR/SLAM Cave Mapper" specifically designed for underwater cave environments, a project he has shared and discussed on the Blue Robotics Community Forums.

While broader open-source initiatives like Hydroffice, Pydro, Kluster, OpenCTD, and FOSSOM research contribute to ocean mapping software and tools, the aforementioned individuals are directly involved in the construction of DIY hardware for underwater cave mapping.

results.response_text:
Divers, engineers, and open-source tech enthusiasts are building DIY underwater cave mapping hardware. Addison Snyder (Free Springs LLC in Gainesville, Florida) is actively developing DIY LiDAR/SLAM mapping rigs. Additionally, open-source projects like the mNemo tool

and divers utilizing DIY acoustic sonar or customized sensor stacks are revolutionizing underground exploration.
Key Innovators & DIY Hardware Projects
Addison Snyder (Free Springs LLC): Based in Florida, Snyder is building a self-contained, open-source LiDAR and SLAM (Simultaneous Localization and Mapping) rig specifically designed to be waterproof and deployed in underwater caves.
Sebastien Kister (Ariane's Line & mNemo): While the mNemo device is an established commercial standard, it was born out of DIY maker roots. It attaches to an exploration guideline and measures distance using tiny spinning wheels alongside an electronic compass and depth gauge.
DIY Acoustic Sonar & Custom Sensor Arrays: Explorers and engineers build custom hardware (often powered by microcontrollers like Arduino and STM32) to map flooded passages where visibility is zero, creating acoustic sensors that act like underwater eyes.
For tips on how to build and integrate DIY acoustic hardware, check out this sonar build demonstration:
Collaborative & DIY Mapping Software
Hardware alone isn't enough; DIY mappers rely on open-source and specialized survey programs. Data is routinely logged, then exported into popular systems to map the environment:
Ariane's Line: A specialized mapping suite developed specifically for cave divers.
Walls & Therion:
Mapping software used by the global caving and cave-diving communities to compile sonar and sensor points into 3D models.
Would you like to explore:
Off-the-shelf components used for amateur acoustic pingers?
3D-modeling software used to convert these DIY hardware scans into models?
Commercial diving protocols
taught by organizations like GUE for survey diving?

Three months drifting through saltwater, waves, storms, and marine life, and it powered on like nothing happened. 🤯🔊 Recovered from the ocean completely covered in barnacles, this JBL speaker still worked normally. Saltwater destroys electronics in days, it corrodes circuits, eats solder joints, and shorts components. So why did this survive? Sealed internal chambers. Waterproof acoustic membranes. Pressure-resistant gaskets. Corrosion-resistant materials. This wasn’t a lab test or a marketing demo. It was real-world punishment from the ocean, and the speaker passed. Engineering \u003e luck. #technology #engineering #innovation #gadgets #durability

queries.prompt: What is the Camazotz cave mapping project?

results.response_text:
The "Camazotz cave mapping project" refers to a suite of browser-based tools primarily designed for technical and cave divers, offering professional dive planning and 3D cave mapping software. It is not a project focused on mapping a specific physical cave, but rather a digital toolset to aid in the surveying and visualization of underwater cave systems.

Key features of the Camazotz diving tools include:
*   **3D Cave Mapping Software** This tool enables the visualization of cave surveys in three dimensions.
*   **Offline Support** The software can function offline within a browser, ensuring usability in remote locations.
*   **Data Export** It supports exporting data to multiple formats.
*   **Advanced Decompression Modeling** In addition to mapping, it provides tools for dive planning, including advanced decompression modeling with algorithms like Buhlmann ZH-L16C and experimental slab diffusion.
*   **Privacy-focused** The tools run entirely in the browser using WebAssembly, with user data remaining on the device, and no accounts or tracking involved.

The name "Camazotz" itself has origins in Mesoamerican mythology, particularly Mayan legends, where Camazotz is a bat god associated with caves, night, death, and sacrifice. This mythological connection likely inspired the naming of the cave diving and mapping software.

results.response_text:
The Camazotz Cave Mapping Project refers to an open-source, web-based software suite built specifically for technical and cave divers. It allows cavers and divers to map underwater and dry cave systems.
The application is completely browser-based and free, allowing users to conduct 3D visualizations of cave surveys, plot passages, and export their findings to multiple formats without needing offline accounts or installation.
Note: If you were referring to "Camazotz" and "caves" in the context of the Netflix series Stranger Things, it actually refers to a psychic dreamscape/prison created by Vecna that the character Holly Wheeler compares to the dark universe in the novel A Wrinkle In Time.
Are you looking for browser-based tools for a personal caving or diving trip, or are you trying to understand the lore behind "Camazotz" in the Stranger Things universe? Let me know and I can get you the exact information you need!

results.response_text:
Camazotz and Ixchel are two distinct deities within Mayan mythology and were not "built" in a literal sense. They are mythological figures with origins in ancient Mesoamerican cultures.

**Camazotz** is a bat spirit or god associated with death, night, and sacrifice in Late Post-Classic Maya mythology. His name translates to "death bat" in the K'iche' language. The concept of a bat deity first appeared around 100 C.E. with the indigenous Zapotec people of Mexico, who believed bats represented death. This deity was later adopted by the K'iche' Maya, who integrated it with their own fire god to form Camazotz. He is often depicted as an anthropomorphic leaf-nosed bat, sometimes holding a sacrificial knife and a human heart. Camazotz is a prominent figure in the Popol Vuh, the sacred text of the K'iche' Maya.

**Ixchel** is the Mayan moon goddess, whose story originated in the Yucatán Peninsula and Guatemala around 1500 BCE. She is a powerful deity with domains over water, fertility, harvests, pregnancy, love, sexuality, medicine, and weaving. Ixchel is depicted in various forms, from a beautiful young maiden to a fierce older woman capable of causing storms. According to myth, she married Itzamná, the god of heaven and wisdom, and together they created the Bacab, who hold up the corners of creation.

results.response_text:
Both Camazotz and Ixchel are fictional planets created by author Madeleine L'Engle in her 1962 science-fantasy novel, A Wrinkle in Time
. In the story, they represent opposite worlds: Camazotz is an oppressed, sterile world controlled by an evil brain called IT, while Ixchel is a warm, lightless planet inhabited by loving, empathetic creatures known as the Beasts.
The names of both planets are borrowed from ancient Mesoamerican figures, though L'Engle used them primarily to fit the novel's mythological and cosmic themes:
Camazotz shares its name with a fearsome, cave-dwelling bat god from the Maya and Zapotec cultures (derived from the Kʼicheʼ Maya words for "death bat")
.
Ixchel is the name of a venerated ancient Maya goddess associated with medicine, midwifery, and weaving.
Would you like to explore Camazotz and Ixchel in the context of the book, or discuss the mythological origins of their names further?

AO THROWBACK - In the Maya culture, the Mesoamerican bat god ...
The Story Behind Camazotz - Reading The Caribbean

Escape from Camazotz! As we all obsessively watch volume two of Stranger Things Season 5, I can’t help but think about the ancient Maya and Camazotz. Camazotz is, of course, in Madeleine L’Engle’s book A Wrinkle in Time as this dark, evil planet controlled by a malevolent force. BUT! Camazotz shares its name with an ancient Maya bat deity known as the “death bat” (in K’iche’ Maya at least) who lived in the underworld. He famously appears in the creation story The Popol Vuh. So did L’Engle borrow from ancient Maya mythology to create her story? And then did it influence the Duffer Brothers when they created Stranger Things (hello, demobats!)?! I can’t help but think YES. If you want to learn more about bats and Camazotz among the ancient Maya, you can find my ARTSQ video on our website (artsq.org) or YouTube channel. Link in bio. @strangerthingstv @strangerthingsnetfliix #arthistorynerd #mesoamerica #bats #camazotz #strangerthings5

references.title: Escape from Camazotz! As we all obsessively watch volume two of Stranger Things Season 5, I can’t help but think about the ancient Maya and Camazotz. Camazotz is, of course, in Madeleine L’Engle’s book A Wrinkle in Time as this dark, evil planet controlled by a malevolent force. BUT! Camazotz shares its name with an ancient Maya bat deity known as the “death bat” (in K’iche’ Maya at least) who lived in the underworld. He famously appears in the creation story The Popol Vuh. So did L’Engle borrow from ancient Maya mythology to create her story? And then did it influence the Duffer Brothers when they created Stranger Things (hello, demobats!)?! I can’t help but think YES. If you want to learn more about bats and Camazotz among the ancient Maya, you can find my ARTSQ video on our website (artsq.org) or YouTube channel. Link in bio. @strangerthingstv @strangerthingsnetfliix #arthistorynerd #mesoamerica #bats #camazotz #strangerthings5

queries.prompt: What is Brickspect and who made it?

results.error: Invalid response body while trying to fetch https://api.openai.com/v1/chat/completions: Premature close
Invalid response body while trying to fetch https://api.anthropic.com/v1/messages: Premature close

results.response_text:
Brickspect is a fully automated vision defect detection system designed for manufacturers of building materials, specifically paving blocks and hollow bricks. It utilizes vision cameras and machine learning algorithms to detect and remove defects such as cracks, discolorations, cavities, and incorrect dimensions on the upper surface of these products. The system also incorporates laser triangulation sensors for accurate height control of construction products. Brickspect aims to optimize production by automating quality control processes, thereby preventing issues and costs associated with defective products later in the supply chain.

Brickspect was made by KSM Vision.
